---
title: "Magnificent Ukraine. U Krymu benzynu katma"
author: "Myron Lubiv"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: true
  message: false
  warning: false
---

```{r}
#Libraries you need
library(tidyverse)
library(maps)
library(ggimage)
 
 # Map of Ukraine
 ukr_map <- map_data("world", region = "Ukraine")
 
 # Locations
 gadienyv <- data.frame(city = "Gadienyv",
                        lon = 22.2879,
                        lat = 48.6208)
 
 serber <- data.frame(city = "Serednii Bereziv",
                      lon = 24.8092,
                      lat = 48.4208)
 
 sevastopol <- data.frame(
   lon = 33.5269,
   lat = 44.6044,
   image = "C:/Users/myron/OneDrive/Documents/Rbasics2026/Basic_R_2026/Sevastopolnb.png"

 )
 
 kyiv <- data_frame(
   lon= 30.5234,
   lat = 50.4501,
   image = "C:/Users/myron/OneDrive/Documents/Rbasics2026/Basic_R_2026/Kyivfco.png"
 )
 
 # Run the code
 ukraine <- ggplot(ukr_map, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group)) +
   geom_polygon(fill = "skyblue", color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
   coord_quickmap() +
   geom_point(data = gadienyv, aes(x = lon, y = lat),
              color = "red", size = 1, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
   geom_text(data = gadienyv, aes(x = lon, y = lat, label = city),
             nudge_x = 0.3, fontface = "bold", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
   geom_point(data = serber, aes(x = lon, y = lat),
              color = "purple", size = 1, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
   geom_text(data = serber, aes(x = lon, y = lat, label = city),
             nudge_x = 0.3, fontface = "bold", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
   geom_image(
     data = sevastopol,
     aes(x = lon, y = lat, image = image),
     inherit.aes = FALSE,
     size = 0.4,
   ) +
   
  geom_image( data = kyiv, 
              aes(x = lon, y = lat, image = image), 
              inherit.aes = FALSE, 
              size = 0.09
              )+
  
   ggtitle("UBDS R basics map",
           subtitle = "July 11–25, 2026") +
   theme(
     plot.title = element_text(color = "#005", face = "bold", size = 13),
     plot.subtitle = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11)
   )
 
 ggsave("ukraine_plot.png", plot = ukraine, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
 
 #Generate the picture
 ukraine
 
       
```
